query: France Ebola case 2026: current status of the doctor who tested positive after aid mission, any secondary cases, ECDC reporting. How many confirmed Ebola cases in France as of July 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is the summary of key findings:

## France Ebola Case 2026 — Key Findings

**Total confirmed Ebola cases in France as of July 2026: ONE (1)**

**The doctor's current status:** RECOVERED and DISCHARGED. French Health Minister Stéphanie Rist announced on July 4, 2026 that the patient recovered and left the hospital following two negative PCR tests. The doctor — a humanitarian physician with the NGO ALIMA — was "almost asymptomatic," experiencing only headaches. The case was confirmed June 24, 2026, after the doctor flew commercially from the DRC arriving June 23.

**Secondary cases:** NONE reported. Five fellow flight passengers were identified as possible contacts and placed in precautionary isolation, with no onward transmission.

**ECDC reporting:** ECDC recorded this as the **first imported Ebola case in Europe** during the 2026 outbreak and assessed the risk of sustained EU/EEA transmission as **"very low."** It urged Member States to continue investing in preparedness and issued contact-tracing guidance.

**Strain/context:** Rare **Bundibugyo strain**, tied to the DRC's Ituri Province outbreak (reported by Africa CDC May 15, 2026), which surged past 1,000 cases (~1,118 by June 26) with at least 452 deaths as of July 3, 2026.

Note: The AskNews news tools hit account limits, but I cross-validated all findings across Euronews, CNN, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, TechTimes, ECDC, and Santé publique France.

sources.title: France: first Ebola case 'recovered' and 'discharged from hospital'
sources.snippet: Health Minister Stéphanie Rist announced the patient recovered and was discharged after two negative PCR tests; patient was almost asymptomatic aside from headaches; five flight passengers placed in isolation as contacts.

sources.title: First Ebola case confirmed in France, health ministry says
sources.snippet: France confirmed its first Ebola case — a doctor returning from a humanitarian mission in the DRC — the first confirmed Ebola case in Europe.

sources.title: Ebola response worker who traveled from DRC to France is first in outbreak to test positive outside of Africa
sources.snippet: First reported case in France and first to test positive outside Africa; outbreak driven by the rare Bundibugyo strain.

sources.title: As first imported Ebola case is recorded in Europe, ECDC calls on EU Member States to continue investing in preparedness
sources.snippet: ECDC states risk of sustained transmission within EU/EEA is very low with effective detection, isolation, and treatment; one imported case in a doctor returning from DRC.

sources.title: Risk classification and contact tracing of travellers returning from affected areas – Ebola disease outbreak 2026 caused by Bundibugyo virus
sources.snippet: ECDC guidance on risk classification and contact tracing for travellers returning from Ebola-affected areas during the 2026 Bundibugyo virus outbreak.

sources.title: Ebola Arrives in France: Infected Aid Doctor Flew Commercial as 1,118 DRC Cases Mount
sources.snippet: France confirmed its first Ebola case June 24, 2026 — an ALIMA physician; DRC outbreak at ~1,118 cases.

sources.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor returning from DR Congo
sources.snippet: Patient in isolation following strict biosafety protocols; contact tracing underway; risk to wider European population low.

sources.title: Our Work – Santé publique France (Ebola)
sources.snippet: Africa CDC reported an Ebola outbreak in Ituri Province, eastern DRC, on May 15, 2026; Bundibugyo Ebola virus identified by National Institute for Biomedical Research in Kinshasa.

sources.title: France's First Ebola Patient Has Recovered, Health Minister Says
sources.snippet: France's first Ebola patient has recovered, health minister announced July 4, 2026.

Negative PCR tests required for patient discharge
